summary:
Volume is down 13% over the last four weeks versus the prior block — 113 km across 8 runs compared to 131 across 10 — which looks like a natural pull-back after the 44 km effort on June 8th absorbed a significant chunk of load. That ultra-distance run at 5:53/km with HR 155 was the anchor of the block, and the reduced run count since reflects appropriate recovery management.

The June 27th run stands out as the quality marker of the recent period: 15.73 km at 4:49/km with HR 161, load 239 — that's your sharpest recent effort and sits meaningfully faster than your current LT pace of 4:24/km at 178 bpm, suggesting you were working hard but not at threshold ceiling. Today's LT update at 4:24/km is a useful new reference point; without prior 30-day history to compare against it's a baseline rather than a trend, but it gives you a concrete anchor for intensity targeting going forward.

Strength is at 4 sessions in the last four weeks — present but thin relative to your 34 YTD across 27 weeks, which averages closer to 1.5 sessions per week; worth pulling that frequency back up now that the post-ultra load has settled. Daily steps at
